_schema-version.description: The schema version that is being used for authoring a descriptor. The schema version should comply with the semantic versioning standard.

ID.description: An application ID, that may contain any number of unicode characters and should be unique in the target runtime environment.

description.description: A free text describing this application.

version.description: The application version that should comply with the semantic versioning standard.

provider.description: The provider or vendor of this application.

copyright.description: A copyright statement from the provider.

name.description: The MTA module name which must be unique and cannot be the same as any provided property set or resource name.

type.description: The module type that defines the design-time tools for the module.

description.description: A free text describing this module.

path.description: The path to a folder that contains the module artifacts.

deployed-after.description: A list of modules that must be deployed before deploying the current module.

properties.description: A collection of key-value pairs that are available to the module at runtime.

properties-metadata.description: Additional information about the module's properties.

parameters.description: Configuration parameters that are used when deploying the module to the target runtime environment.

parameters-metadata.description: Additional information about the module's parameters.

hooks.description: A list of hooks that will be executed for the module.

build-parameters.description: The build parameters define how the module is built by the corresponding tool.

includes.description: It is possible to define parameters in external files. The "includes" section is used to point to those files.

name.description: The name of an "includes" section. This name is used by a builder to generate a parameter section in the deployment descriptor.

path.description: A path pointing to a file that contains a map of parameters, either in JSON or in YAML format.

provides.description: List of provided names (MTA internal) to which properties can be attached

requires.description: List of names either matching a resource name or a name provided by another module within the same MTA that are required by this module.

resources.description: A list of resources that are required by the modules of this MTA project.

name.description: The resource name which must be unique and cannot be the same as any provided property set or module name.

type.description: A type of resource supported by deployment tools in the target environment.

description.description: A free text describing this resource.

properties.description: A collection of key-value pairs that can be required by modules and/or other resources as configuration data at runtime.

properties-metadata.description: Additional information about the resource's properties.

parameters.description: Configuration parameters that are used when provisioning the resource in the target runtime environment.

parameters-metadata.description: Additional information about the resource's parameters.

includes.description: It is possible to define parameters in external files. The "includes" section is used to point to those files.

name.description: The name of an "includes" section. This name is used by a builder to generate a parameter section in the deployment descriptor.

path.description: A path pointing to a file that contains a map of parameters, either in JSON or in YAML format.

optional.description: If the resource is set to optional and the deployment tools are unable to allocate it, then the tools will issue a warning and continue the deployment process.

processed-after.description: A list containing the names of the resources that must be processed prior to this one.

active.description: If a resource is declared to be inactive, the resource will not be allocated during the deployment process.

requires.description: A list of property sets provided by other modules and/or resources (providers) that this resource requires.

name.description: The name of a provided property set or resource (provider) that this resource requires.

properties.description: Resource properties whose values are determined by the provided properties. These properties can be referenced by the tilde notation: "~{<provided-property-name>}"

properties-metadata.description: Additional information about the required properties.

parameters.description: Configuration parameters that are used when provisioning the resource in the target runtime environment.

parameters-metadata.description: Additional information about the required parameters.

includes.description: It is possible to define parameters in external files. The "includes" section is used to point to those files.

name.description: The name of an "includes" section. This name is used by a builder to generate a parameter section in the deployment descriptor.

path.description: A path pointing to a file that contains a map of parameters, either in JSON or in YAML format.

parameters.description: Global parameters that are used when building or deploying the application

parameters-metadata.description: Additional information about the global parameters.

includes.description: It is possible to define parameters in external files. The "includes" section is used to point to those files.

name.description: The name of an "includes" section. This name is used by a builder to generate a parameter section in the deployment descriptor.

path.description: A path pointing to a file that contains a map of parameters, either in JSON or in YAML format.

includes.description: It is possible to define parameters in external files. The "includes" section is used to point to those files.

name.description: The name of an "includes" section. This name is used by a builder to generate a parameter section in the deployment descriptor.

path.description: A path pointing to a file that contains a map of parameters, either in JSON or in YAML format.
